title: Standards for protection against radiation
text: Annual occupational dose limits — whole body 5 rem (50 mSv), lens of eye 15 rem, skin/extremity 50 rem. Public dose limit 100 mrem/yr (1 mSv). ALARA principle. Posting + labeling of radiation areas. Records retention 75 years for occupational. Effluent reporting per Appendix B.

title: Immediate notification requirements
text: Licensees must notify NRC Operations Center: 1-hour reports — declaration of emergency, accidental criticality, fire/explosion damaging safety system; 4-hour reports — initiation of any nuclear plant shutdown required by tech specs, ECCS discharge, RPS actuation; 8-hour reports — degraded condition + unplanned PWR rod position. Use ENS line 301-816-5100.

title: Licensee Event Report (LER)
text: Written LER required within 60 days for: completion of any nuclear plant shutdown required by tech specs; operation/condition prohibited by tech specs; unanalyzed condition that significantly degrades safety; ECCS actuation; common-cause failure of safety system; loss of safety function. Submit via Form 366 + 366A continuation.

title: Quality Assurance Criteria
text: 18 QA criteria for safety-related SSCs: I Organization, II QA Program, III Design Control, IV Procurement Document Control, V Instructions/Procedures/Drawings, VI Document Control, VII Control of Purchased Material/Equipment/Services, VIII Identification + Control of Materials, IX Control of Special Processes, X Inspection, XI Test Control, XII Control of M+TE, XIII Handling/Storage/Shipping, XIV Inspection/Test/Operating Status, XV Nonconforming Materials, XVI Corrective Action, XVII QA Records, XVIII Audits.

title: Physical protection of nuclear power reactors
text: Licensee must establish + maintain a physical protection program including: protected area (double fence + intrusion detection); vital area + central + secondary alarm stations; armed response team (24/7); access authorization + behavioral observation; vehicle barriers (DBT); insider mitigation. Force-on-Force exercises every 3 years per NRC.

title: Material control + accounting of Special Nuclear Material (SNM)
text: Licensees with formula quantity SNM (Category I — >5kg HEU equivalent) must: physical inventory every 2-6 months; item monitoring; material balance accounting with limit of error of inventory difference (LEID); shipping/receiving tamper-indicating devices; report inventory differences exceeding LEID. Cat II + III have proportionally lower requirements.

title: Comprehensive safeguards agreement (NPT states)
text: State commitment to apply IAEA safeguards to all source/special fissionable material in peaceful activities. Provides: nuclear material accountancy as the fundamental safeguards measure; complementary use of containment + surveillance; designs IAEA inspections + verifications. Additional Protocol (INFCIRC/540) expands declarations + access.

title: Nuclear Security Recommendations on Physical Protection
text: Physical protection objectives: protect against unauthorized removal of nuclear material in use/storage/transport; protect against sabotage of nuclear material + nuclear facilities. Risk-informed graded approach by Category (I/II/III). Defense-in-depth — multiple layers of physical protection.

title: Equipment Reliability Process
text: Continuous improvement process for high-reliability operation: scope determination (critical components); performance monitoring (parameters indicating degradation); continuous equipment reliability improvement (corrective actions); life-cycle management (replacement strategy); preventive maintenance basis (vendor + experience-informed).

title: ROP — significance determination + action matrix
text: Findings characterized by significance: Green (very low safety significance), White (low to moderate), Yellow (substantial), Red (high). Cornerstones: Initiating Events, Mitigating Systems, Barrier Integrity, Emergency Preparedness, Occupational Radiation Safety, Public Radiation Safety, Security. Action matrix specifies NRC + licensee actions by color/PI.
